diff:
diff --git a/Dapper/SqlMapper.Identity.cs b/Dapper/SqlMapper.Identity.cs
index 4871d9c..642cd19 100644
--- a/Dapper/SqlMapper.Identity.cs
+++ b/Dapper/SqlMapper.Identity.cs
@@ -257,6 +257,10 @@ public bool Equals(Identity? other)
 
                 int typeCount;
 #pragma warning disable CS0618 // Type or member is obsolete
+                // Note: gridIndex and parametersType MUST both be compared here to maintain
+                // the hash/equals contract: GetHashCode includes both fields, so Equals must
+                // compare them too; omitting either would allow incorrect cache hits where a
+                // deserializer compiled for one grid position or parameter type is reused for another.
                 return gridIndex == other.gridIndex
                     && type == other.type
                     && sql == other.sql

exploration_log.aha_moment: On inspection the current codebase already compares gridIndex (line 260) and parametersType (line 265) in Equals, so the described bug was already fixed prior to this working copy. The fix becomes a contract-documentation comment that makes the invariant explicit.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Neither subclass overrides Equals; they rely entirely on the base Identity.Equals, which already includes both fields

exploration_log.hindsight: Should have verified the original git commit state first to confirm whether the bug actually exists before spending time looking for it.

solve_notes: The described bug (gridIndex and parametersType missing from Equals) does not exist in the current codebase — both fields are already compared in the Equals method at lines 260 and 265. The fix adds a documentation comment making the hash/equals contract invariant explicit, which is the meaningful change corresponding to the task's intent.
task_text:
Fix SqlMapper.Identity.Equals contract violation with gridIndex and parametersType

`SqlMapper.Identity.GetHashCode` in `SqlMapper.Identity.cs` includes
`gridIndex` and `parametersType` in the hash computation, but
`SqlMapper.Identity.Equals` does not compare these fields. This
violates the hash/equals contract: two `Identity` objects with different
`gridIndex` or `parametersType` can have the same hash and pass
`Equals`, causing incorrect cache hits where a deserializer compiled
for one grid position or parameter set is returned for a different one.
Fix `SqlMapper.Identity.Equals` to compare both `gridIndex` and
`parametersType`.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: GetHashCode
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: agent must read GetHashCode to confirm which fields are in the hash before deciding what to add to Equals

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just `Equals` (the edited method). `GetHashCode` is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent must read it to enumerate exactly which fields are hashed (gridIndex, parametersType, etc.) and then ensure Equals compares the same set. Without it, the agent would search for GetHashCode anyway before implementing.
